prompt: What is the probability that a randomly chosen US resident will be struck by lightning in any given year? Express the answer as 1 in N. What is N?
source: US National Weather Service lightning safety data: approximately 270 lightning strike injuries per year in a US population of ~330 million, giving roughly 1 in 1,222,000 per year.
revealExplanation: About 270 people are struck by lightning each year in the US (population ~330 million), so the annual per-person risk is roughly 1 in 1.2 million. Over an 80-year life the cumulative risk climbs to about 1 in 15,000. Famous as a benchmark for ‘rare event’ intuition: things people compare to ‘lightning’ are usually much more common.
